creator: Alexander Terp
description:
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-14 (rad v0.10.1): still a raw Go panic.

Writing an integer literal that exceeds int64 range causes an unrecoverable Go panic instead of a user-friendly error.

**Repro:**
x = 9223372036854775808   // max int64 + 1
// panic: converter: failed to parse int "9223372036854775808": strconv.ParseInt: ... value out of range

Also cannot express the minimum int64 value as a literal: -9223372036854775808 panics.

**Source:** `rts/converter.go:1212` (was cited as :1151) - `convertInt()` calls `panic()` on parse failure.

**Fix (slam dunk):** replace the `panic()` with a clean rad compile error; the converter already emits errors elsewhere, so callers handle it. Kills a raw Go panic.
title: Int literal overflow causes Go panic
